compartments.label: Lumen
compartments.snippet: Labeled region upper left, above brush-border microvilli; contains P. aeruginosa, Pyocyanin, Pyoverdine, ExoA, PCN, FSHR-1

compartments.label: Intestinal cell
compartments.snippet: Large outer box labeled 'Intestinal cell' bottom left; the main cytoplasmic compartment containing most pathway nodes

compartments.label: Mitochondria
compartments.snippet: Teal organelle drawn center of intestinal cell; contains UPR^mt and mtROS labels

compartments.label: Nucleus
compartments.snippet: Large reddish oval lower center of intestinal cell; contains ESRE, cellular stress response genes, antimicrobial defence genes, ZIP-2 at boundary

genes.snippet: Blue transmembrane receptor glyph on right side of Lumen/cell border
Listed as TIR-1/SARM1 yellow/cytoplasmic node in Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Yellow oval labeled NSY-1/MAPKKK in Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Yellow oval labeled SEK-1/MAPKK in Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Yellow oval labeled PMK-1/p38 MAPK in Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Blue oval in Intestinal cell cytoplasm, receives arrow from Mitochondria UPR^mt arm
Purple box labeled ATFS-1/ATF-4 in Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Purple box in Intestinal cell cytoplasm, receives uncertain arrow from mtROS
Purple box in Intestinal cell cytoplasm, receives arrow from mtROS
Purple box labeled DAF-16/FOXO in Intestinal cell cytoplasm; uncertain link from PMK-1
Purple box labeled SKN-1/Nrf2 in Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Purple box in Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Purple box in Intestinal cell cytoplasm, downstream of PMK-1
Purple box right side of Intestinal cell cytoplasm, feeds ESRE
Listed as CEBP-1/2 purple box right side of Intestinal cell cytoplasm
Purple box straddling Nucleus boundary, feeds ESRE and antimicrobial defence genes
Purple box labeled NHR-86/HNF-4 at bottom right on nucleus/cell edge

tentative_edges.snippet: Dashed arrow: Pyocyanin hexagon in Lumen → Pyocyanin in Intestinal cell (transport)
Pyocyanin (cell) → TIR-1/SARM1; pointed arrow
TIR-1/SARM1 → LRO stress/pH increase; pointed arrow. LRO stress is a process/organelle node, not a gene.
NSY-1 (MAPKKK) → SEK-1 (MAPKK); pointed arrow in canonical p38 cascade
SEK-1 (MAPKK) → PMK-1 (p38 MAPK); pointed arrow
PMK-1 → ATF-7; pointed arrow
ATF-7 → antimicrobial defence genes (into nucleus); pointed arrow
PMK-1 → DAF-16/FOXO; marked as uncertain link in transcription notes [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
DAF-16/FOXO → cellular stress response genes (into nucleus)
SKN-1/Nrf2 → cellular stress response genes (into nucleus)
HSF-1 → cellular stress response genes / antimicrobial defence genes; overlapping arrowheads noted as partly ambiguous
HSF-1 --- ROS box; relationship described as faint/associated in transcription [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
Dashed arrow: Pyoverdine in Lumen → Pyoverdine in Intestinal cell (transport)
Pyoverdine (cell) → Mitochondria iron (+Fe³⁺); pointed arrow indicating iron chelation/delivery
Mitochondria UPR^mt → HSP-60; leftward pointed arrow from mitochondria
HSP-60 → nucleus (cellular stress response genes); marked as uncertain in transcription [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
ATFS-1/ATF-4 → nucleus (cellular stress response genes); pointed arrow, slightly ambiguous convergence
mtROS → HIF-1; uncertain arrow from mitochondria mtROS label
HIF-1 → ESRE / nucleus genes; pointed arrow
mtROS → AMPK; pointed arrow from mitochondria mtROS
AMPK → antimicrobial defence genes; uncertain target noted in transcription
Dashed arrow: ExoA in Lumen → ExoA in Intestinal cell (transport)
ExoA --| Translation; clear T-bar inhibition arrow in figure [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
FSHR-1 receptor → dashed arrow marked '?' downward into cell signaling; unresolved/hypothetical link per figure
Dashed arrow: PCN hexagon in Lumen → toward ZIP-2/ESRE module in cell
ZIP-4 → ESRE (nucleus); pointed arrow [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
CEBP-1/2 → ESRE (nucleus); pointed arrow [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
ZIP-2 → ESRE and antimicrobial defence genes (into nucleus); pointed arrows [unconfirmed against figure in verification]
NHR-86/HNF-4 → antimicrobial defence genes (into nucleus); pointed arrow
ESRE → cellular stress response genes and antimicrobial defence genes within nucleus; pointed arrows
P. aeruginosa in lumen produces Pyocyanin, Pyoverdine, ExoA, PCN as toxic effectors targeting intestinal cell
